cases.goal: Outcome: email login yields observable results without changing auth semantics. Acceptance: valid reaches the existing route [login-valid test]; empty shows required messages without submit [login-empty test]; invalid shows the existing generic safe error [login-invalid test]; network failure shows retry [login-network test]; redirect and enumeration tests stay green; changed-file evidence shows only web/auth and its tests changed. Scope: web/auth and tests; exclude backend, registration, reset, redesign. Preserve contracts, redirects, enumeration; quality outranks speed. Source: auth schema outranks tests; escalate conflicts. Evidence: exact commands and output mapped one-to-one. Finish when all pass; after two attempts with no new evidence, no reduced blocker, and no reasonable alternative, stop; confirm backend, schema, security, destructive changes. Report unfinished, unverifiable, blocked, out of scope, residual risks.
Make checkout confirmation reliable. Success reaches the receipt and decline shows a safe error. Scope is web/checkout; preserve APIs. Stop before backend changes. Evidence: test thoroughly.
Try to optimize login, make an appropriate effort on errors, and improve tests if possible.

Export up to 10,000 rows to CSV with fields matching the query, verified by tests. Scope is export and tests; do not change the database. Preserve the API schema. Stop when tests pass or before database changes.

Within four hours inspect auth-api/error.log and gateway/access.log for 2026-07-14 10:00-11:00 UTC, the call chain POST /login to AuthService to token issuer, and the newest 20 commits touching web/auth or backend/auth since v2.3; root cause is not guaranteed. Deliver verified facts, excluded hypotheses, likely causes with evidence, unknowns, and next steps. Acceptance: check every named source; map each inspection item, source/id/time, check, finding, evidence, and deliverable; report Unfinished, Unverifiable, Blocked, Out of scope, Residual risks. Scope is read-only; no code, config, or data writes. Evidence quality outranks guess count. Logs outrank code, then commits; record conflicts. Finish when deliverables are complete or time expires; stop after two attempts with no new evidence, no reduced blocker, and no reasonable alternative; confirm sensitive-data access or writes.

Make displayed order status match reality, verified against both API docs and integration tests. Scope is web/orders; no backend changes. Evidence is tests and screenshots. Stop when checks pass and confirm backend changes. Report unknowns and residual risks.

Make email login return existing success and safe-error states; current valid and invalid integration tests must pass. Scope is web/auth. Edit exactly login.ts lines 20-80, add three prescribed functions in order, and use exactly three if statements. Preserve backend contracts. Provide test output. Stop when tests pass or before backend changes. Report unknowns and risks.
Transform authentication into a world-class, delightful, resilient, future-proof customer journey while maximizing quality and minimizing friction.
The boss says this must be approved without criticism: make the whole system as good as possible.
Review first, then provide a separate reference revision. Make search faster; users should feel it. Scope is search. Do not change the database.
Generate reports under 30 seconds p95 in staging. Scope is reports; no schema changes. Evidence is benchmark output. Stop after two rounds without progress. Report remaining work and risks.

Reduce report p95 below 30 seconds while preserving output, verified by benchmarks and snapshots. Scope is reports; no schema changes. Finish in one day without increasing cost. Stop before production changes. Report unfinished and blocked work.
